sourceMovie.title: Eat Pray Love
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Eat Pray Love (2010) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ryan Murphy's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Call Me by Your Name
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eat Pray Love clicked for you, Call Me by Your Name (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eat Pray Love?
faq.answer: Try Call Me by Your Name, The Theory of Everything, What's Eating Gilbert Grape—each shares DNA with Eat Pray Love while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eat Pray Love?
faq.answer: Eat Pray Love (2010) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eat Pray Love worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
